welcome.subtitle: AI-powered Cloud Operations Assistant
welcome.hint: Select an agent to start a conversation

network.name: Network Diagnostics Agent
network.description: AWS network diagnostics — VPC, DNS, Flow Logs, NAT Gateway, ELB analysis.
network.placeholder: Enter a network diagnostic request... (e.g., Check VPC subnet connectivity)

dns.name: DNS Diagnostics
dns.prompt: Please check Route 53 hosted zones and record status. Also verify health check status and DNS resolution results.
followUps: Which health checks are currently failing?
Show me the TTL settings for all records
Are there any DNS resolution latency issues?

vpc.name: VPC Config Analysis
vpc.prompt: Analyze the VPC, subnets, routing tables, and Internet/NAT gateway configuration in the current region. Also check security group rules.
followUps: Are there any overly permissive security group rules?
Show me unused or unattached network interfaces
Which subnets have the most available IP addresses?

flowlogs.name: Flow Logs Analysis
flowlogs.prompt: Analyze rejected traffic patterns in VPC Flow Logs. Identify the most frequently blocked source IPs and ports.
followUps: Which security groups are causing the most rejections?
Show me the trend of rejected traffic over the last 24 hours
Are any of the blocked IPs from known internal services?

elb.name: Load Balancer Metrics
elb.prompt: Check ALB/NLB status in the current region. Analyze response times, active connections, and processed bytes.
followUps: Which target groups have unhealthy targets?
Show me the 5xx error rate trend
Are there any load balancers with uneven traffic distribution?

incident.name: Incident Analysis Agent
incident.description: Automated incident analysis — Datadog, OpenSearch, Container Insight integration.
incident.placeholder: Describe the incident... (e.g., API error rate exceeded 5%)

cpu.name: CPU Spike Analysis
cpu.prompt: CPU utilization has spiked in the EKS cluster. A chaos-cpu-stress pod appears to have been deployed. Please analyze the cluster status and root cause.
followUps: Which pods are consuming the most CPU right now?
Show me the CPU usage trend for the last hour
What remediation actions do you recommend?

error.name: Error Rate Increase
error.prompt: ERROR logs (ECONNREFUSED) are surging in the web-api service. Please analyze logs and metrics to determine if chaos-error-injection pod is the cause.
followUps: What is the current error rate percentage?
Which downstream services are affected?
Show me the error log patterns from the last 30 minutes

latency.name: Latency Spike
latency.prompt: Response latency (500-1000ms) is surging in the api-gateway service. Please check container status to determine if chaos-latency-injection pod is the cause.
followUps: What is the P99 latency right now?
Which endpoints have the highest latency?
Are there any resource throttling issues?

pod.name: Pod Restart Loop
pod.prompt: Pods are repeatedly restarting (CrashLoopBackOff) in the EKS cluster. Please diagnose including the chaos-pod-crash pod.
followUps: Show me the recent events for the crashing pods
What are the OOMKilled or exit code details?
Which nodes are the affected pods scheduled on?

incident-cached.name: Incident Agent (Cached)
incident-cached.description: Incident analysis agent (Prompt Cache ON) — for caching performance comparison.

cpu.name: CPU Spike Analysis
cpu.prompt: CPU utilization has spiked in the EKS cluster. A chaos-cpu-stress pod appears to have been deployed. Please analyze the cluster status and root cause.
followUps: Which pods are consuming the most CPU right now?
Show me the CPU usage trend for the last hour
What remediation actions do you recommend?

error.name: Error Rate Increase
error.prompt: ERROR logs (ECONNREFUSED) are surging in the web-api service. Please analyze logs and metrics to determine if chaos-error-injection pod is the cause.
followUps: What is the current error rate percentage?
Which downstream services are affected?
Show me the error log patterns from the last 30 minutes

latency.name: Latency Spike
latency.prompt: Response latency (500-1000ms) is surging in the api-gateway service. Please check container status to determine if chaos-latency-injection pod is the cause.
followUps: What is the P99 latency right now?
Which endpoints have the highest latency?
Are there any resource throttling issues?

pod.name: Pod Restart Loop
pod.prompt: Pods are repeatedly restarting (CrashLoopBackOff) in the EKS cluster. Please diagnose including the chaos-pod-crash pod.
followUps: Show me the recent events for the crashing pods
What are the OOMKilled or exit code details?
Which nodes are the affected pods scheduled on?

k8s.name: K8s Diagnostics Agent
k8s.description: EKS cluster diagnostics — dynamically switching regions for analysis.

health.name: Cluster Health Check
health.prompt: Please check the overall status of the EKS cluster. Verify node status, pod count, resource utilization, and report any anomalies.
followUps: Are there any nodes in NotReady state?
Which namespaces have the most pods?
Show me the cluster events from the last hour

pods.name: Unhealthy Pod Diagnostics
pods.prompt: Find pods in CrashLoopBackOff, Error, or Pending state in the cluster and diagnose the root cause.
followUps: Show me the logs for the crashing pods
Are there any resource limit issues causing OOMKilled?
Which images are failing to pull?

resources.name: Resource Usage Analysis
resources.prompt: Analyze CPU and memory usage across cluster nodes and pods. Check for resource shortages or over-allocated workloads.
followUps: Which pods are using the most memory?
Are any nodes at risk of resource exhaustion?
Show me pods without resource limits set

workloads.name: Workload Overview
workloads.prompt: Show the Deployment, StatefulSet, and DaemonSet status per namespace. Include replica counts and availability status.
followUps: Which deployments have unavailable replicas?
Are there any pending rollouts?
Show me the HPA configuration for scaled workloads

istio.name: Istio Mesh Diagnostics Agent
istio.description: Istio service mesh diagnostics — mTLS, traffic routing, control plane, Envoy sidecar analysis.
istio.placeholder: Enter an Istio mesh diagnostic request... (e.g., 503 error from productpage to reviews)

connectivity.name: Service Connectivity Failure
connectivity.prompt: In the istio-sample namespace, requests from productpage to reviews return 503 errors. Please check topology, sidecars, VirtualService, and mTLS configuration.
followUps: Are the Envoy sidecars running on all affected pods?
Show me the VirtualService and DestinationRule for reviews
Is mTLS STRICT mode causing the connection failures?

mtls.name: mTLS Audit
mtls.prompt: Please check the mTLS configuration status across the entire mesh. Report PeerAuthentication policies, pods without sidecar injection, and security recommendations for all namespaces including retail-store and istio-sample.
followUps: Which pods are missing sidecar injection?
Show me the PeerAuthentication policies per namespace
What steps are needed to enforce STRICT mTLS everywhere?

canary.name: Canary Deployment Analysis
canary.prompt: Please check the traffic routing status for the reviews service in the istio-sample namespace. Compare the VirtualService weight settings (v1=80%, v2=10%, v3=10%) with actual traffic ratios.
followUps: Is the actual traffic ratio matching the configured weights?
Show me the error rate for each version
What would you recommend for the next rollout step?

controlplane.name: Control Plane Status
controlplane.prompt: Please check the istiod control plane status. Report xDS push latency, errors, configuration conflicts, and the number of connected proxies.
followUps: Are there any configuration conflicts or rejections?
What is the current xDS push latency?
How many proxies are connected vs expected?

latency.name: Latency Hotspot Detection
latency.prompt: Scan P99 latency across both retail-store and istio-sample namespaces and identify the slowest services. Also check for VirtualService fault injection.
followUps: Which service-to-service calls have the highest latency?
Are there any active fault injection rules?
Show me the latency breakdown for the slowest service
